message:
feat(fr-conformance): r73v sub-task (a) — re-emit + --loadrdb cross-impl gate (br-frankenredis-r73v)

Lands the file-level cross-implementation round-trip gate the parent
br-frankenredis-r73v calls for in step 1: a `dump.rdb` produced by
upstream redis-server, decoded via `fr_persist::decode_rdb`, then
re-encoded via `fr_persist::encode_rdb`, must load cleanly into a
freshly-spawned upstream redis-server and yield the same in-memory
state.

* New test `live_redis_rdb_reemit_loadrdb_matches_upstream`:
  spawn oracle_a, populate string/list/set/hash/zset shapes (thresholds
  pinned so they hit the canonical type tags fr-persist emits), capture
  pre-state, SAVE, decode + re-encode + write to a fresh data dir,
  spawn oracle_b at that dir (which auto-loads our re-emitted dump.rdb
  on boot), capture post-state, assert equivalence on
  GET / LLEN / LRANGE / SCARD / SMEMBERS (sorted) / HGETALL (sorted-by-field)
  / ZRANGE WITHSCORES.
* `VendoredRedis::spawn_in_dir` accepts an externally-owned data
  directory so the gate can target a pre-populated `dump.rdb`. The
  boot deadline is bumped to 6s to absorb startup loadrdb time.
* The shared `spawn_with` body lifts the port allocation out of `spawn`,
  so the two callers can't race on the same ephemeral port.
* This PR also rolls in mid-flight RdbStreamMetadata wiring on the
  fr-persist + fr-store + fr-runtime + fuzz/metamorphic side (other
  agent's stream byte-identical re-emit groundwork) so the workspace
  builds; minimal patches in fr-runtime and the fuzz example follow
  the new 4-tuple `RdbValue::Stream(entries, watermark, groups, metadata)`
  shape.

Streams (RDB_TYPE_STREAM=15 fr-persist private encoding) and
HashWithTtls (type 100, FrankenRedis private) are excluded from this
gate — their cross-impl loadrdb story rides on br-frankenredis-ian4
(file-level stream corpus + STREAM_LISTPACKS_3 byte-identical re-emit).

Validated via rch: cargo check --workspace --all-targets clean,
cargo test -p fr-conformance --lib green (187/187), cargo clippy
-p fr-conformance --all-targets -- -D warnings clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
